_note: D3.C.2 failure-mode example #3. Legacy UK office major refurbishment with 50W GU10 halogen downlights (750 initial lm, 15 lm/W lamp efficacy). is_uk_new_build=true on a major refurb triggers Part L 2021 §6 enforcement. 50 fittings in 5×10 grid maintain 343 lux (INV-1 PASS via brute-force fitting count) but lamp efficacy 15 lm/W ≪ 95 lm/W Part L target → INV-6 FAIL critical. Three non_compliance_flags entries with severity=critical. INV-10 composite FAIL. This is the canonical 'inherited halogen estate' refurb where the engineer needs three changes (efficacy + daylight zoning + occupancy) before Part L sign-off.

luminaire_type.description: 50W GU10 halogen recessed downlight — legacy refurbishment (LED_DOWNLIGHT ontology used for photometric geometry proxy; lumen + efficacy values are the actual halogen specification)

selection_source.citation: CIBSE LG7 §6.2 (recessed downlight typical values; narrow beam) + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 — LED_DOWNLIGHT ontology used as photometric geometry proxy for the halogen fitting (similar 100 mm aperture, similar narrow beam optic). Lumen + efficacy fields override ontology defaults with the actual halogen specification.

zones.label: Interior — legacy halogen estate (no perimeter zoning despite W glazing)

calculation_summary.ugr_status: UGR > 19 likely — narrow-beam halogen downlight at 1:1 density typically produces UGR 22-25 at desk plane; confirm from photometric data

non_compliance_flags.message: Lamp efficacy 15 lm/W ≪ 95 lm/W Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) §6 Table 6.2 target — 50W GU10 halogen non-compliant for new-build / major-refurb commercial. Remediation: replace with 8W GU10 LED retrofit lamp at 800 lm (100 lm/W) keeping same fitting + transformer; OR full luminaire-replacement with LED_DOWNLIGHT 10W/1000 lm canonical (100 lm/W) cutting connected load from 2500 W to 500 W (80 % energy saving).
non_compliance_flags.reference: Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) §6 Table 6.2 + BS EN 15193-1:2017 § 6

non_compliance_flags.message: controls.daylight_linking=false despite inputs.glazed_walls=['west'] — Part L 2021 §6 requires daylight-linked dimming on luminaires within 6 m of glazed wall. Remediation: split column 0 (L01, L11, L21, L31, L41 at x=300, all within 1500 mm of W wall) into perimeter zone Z1 on 0-10V dimming or DALI with photocell feedback.
non_compliance_flags.reference: Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) § 6 + BS EN 15193-1:2017 § 6 + [control-rules#part-l-daylight]

non_compliance_flags.message: controls.occupancy_sensing=false on a new-build / major-refurb private office — Part L 2021 §6 requires automatic occupancy detection (presence-with-absence-detection) for office spaces. Remediation: add a PIR sensor wired to the C-L02..C-L05 interior circuits with manual override via SW01.

assumptions: UF = 0.55 from LED_DOWNLIGHT ontology proxy at RI=1.5, reflectances 0.7/0.5/0.2 — halogen and LED downlight share similar 100 mm aperture geometry and narrow beam optic, so UF table is a reasonable proxy. Engineer must confirm against the actual halogen photometric file before issue.

MF = 0.80 with LLMF generous (halogen LLMF degrades faster than LED; a stricter assessment would use 0.70). Achieved value would drop from 343 to 301 lux at MF=0.70 — still ≥ 300 target but with no margin.
Initial lumens 750 used at face value; design lumens after burn-in would be ~600 (halogen depreciates 20 % in first 200 h).
Engineer over-provisioned to 50 fittings on a 48 m² floor (1 fitting/m²) — typical inherited halogen 'wash' density. INV-1 PASSes by brute-force fitting count, not by efficient design.

v1.6.0 → v1.7.0 D5 retrofit: zone Z2 defaults to purpose='task' (ZP-01); all 50 GU10 halogen recessed downlights default to mount_type='recessed' (MT-01); per_zone_achieved[] populated from existing achieved_illuminance_lux=343.75 lx (Z2 task target=300, achieved=343, ratio_compliance=pass). Defaults preserve v1.6.0 behaviour — no change to lumen-method calc, layout, controls, or the Part-L FAIL state (INV-6/INV-10 FAIL + 3 critical non_compliance_flags + part_l_compliant=false all preserved verbatim). D5 additions are orthogonal to Part-L compliance — photometric INV-19 PASS coexists with regulatory INV-6 FAIL.

drawing_notes: All luminaires: 50W GU10 halogen recessed downlight, 750 initial lm, 2800K (warm), CRI 100, IP20
NON-COMPLIANT LAYOUT — DO NOT ISSUE FOR PART L SIGN-OFF (lamp efficacy 15 lm/W ≪ 95 lm/W target + no automatic controls)
Remediation path: GU10 LED retrofit (8W / 800 lm / 100 lm/W) keeps fitting + transformer + cabling but cuts connected load 84 %
5 row circuits C-L01..C-L05 on DB L1; 10 fittings × 50 W = 500 W per circuit on 6A Type B MCB (45 % of 1104 W 80 % headroom — limiting factor is lamp-load, not OCPD)
Total connected lighting load 2500 W in a 48 m² private office — 52 W/m² density (vs Part L 2021 NIA reference 8 W/m²)
Single 1-gang switch SW01 at S entrance latch side, 1200 mm AFF — no occupancy or daylight automation
Glazed W wall (windows) present but no perimeter daylight zone declared — Part L gap
Ceiling grid not specified — confirm against existing reflected ceiling plan during survey
flags: NON-COMPLIANCE: Part L lamp efficacy critical (INV-6) — 15 lm/W vs 95 lm/W target
NON-COMPLIANCE: Part L no daylight-linking (INV-6) — glazed wall + dim controls absent
NON-COMPLIANCE: Part L no occupancy detection (INV-6) — manual switch only

rationale.chat_summary: Legacy 8×6 m UK private office major refurbishment with inherited 50W GU10 halogen downlights — 50 fittings in 5×10 grid maintain 343 lux (INV-1 PASSes by brute-force density) but lamp efficacy 15 lm/W ≪ 95 lm/W Part L 2021 §6 target → INV-6 FAIL critical. Plus no daylight-linking despite glazed W wall + no occupancy detection — three severity=critical non_compliance_flags entries. Remediation: GU10 LED retrofit (8W / 800 lm) + perimeter daylight zone + PIR. INV-10 composite FAIL.

sections.title: Why this example exists
sections.summary: Demonstrates the canonical 'inherited halogen estate' refurb where the engineer-of-record gets handed a working but non-compliant existing layout and needs to surface the Part L gap before signing off. Three severity=critical non_compliance_flags entries exercise INV-6 sub-checks 2, 3, 4 simultaneously (the only INV-6 sub-check that PASSes is sub-check 1: controls.part_l_assessed=true — the engineer DID assess Part L, just got a fail). INV-10 composite FAIL flags the layout-level non-compliance honestly.

sections.title: Lumen-method walk (PASS by brute-force fitting count)
sections.summary: A=48 m². Hm=1950 mm. RI=1.76 → table key 1.5. UF=0.55 (LED_DOWNLIGHT ontology proxy at RI=1.5; halogen GU10 has similar 100 mm aperture + narrow beam optic). MF=0.80 (generous; halogen LLMF degrades faster than LED — stricter assessment would use 0.70 giving achieved 301 lux). Engineer specified N=50 (1 fitting per m² — typical inherited halogen 'wash' density). Achieved = (50×750×0.55×0.80)/48 = 16500/48 = 343.75 lux ≥ 300 target → INV-1 PASS with 14.6 % headroom. The PASS is real but unprincipled — 50 halogen fittings consume 2500 W of connected lighting load (52 W/m² density vs Part L 2021 reference 8 W/m²). The engineer over-provisioned fittings to compensate for halogen's low efficacy.

decisions.label: Lux target 300 lx maintained
decisions.summary: BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.3 entry for private_office sets 300-500 lx maintained depending on task. Using 300 lx as the lower bound — refurbishment retains the inherited 'general office lighting' brief rather than the higher 500 lx task-focused target.

decisions.label: N=50 by inherited fitting count (not lumen-method derived)
decisions.summary: The existing halogen estate has 50 fittings in a 5×10 grid. Engineer chose to retain the layout (refurbishment, not new build) and over-provision rather than re-lay out. INV-1 PASSes but the design is energy-inefficient by design.
decisions.code_clause: Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) § 6 + CIBSE TM 50:2020 retrofit guidance

sections.title: Part L assessment — three critical failures (INV-6)
sections.summary: inputs.new_build=true (major refurbishment triggers Part L 2021 §6 per Approved Doc L Vol 2 §0.4) AND jurisdiction=england_wales triggers Part L enforcement. Four sub-checks per validator.md INV-6: (1) controls.part_l_assessed=true PASS — the engineer did run the assessment. (2) controls.required=[] does NOT include 'occupancy' FAIL critical. (3) inputs.glazed_walls=['west'] non-empty AND controls.required does NOT include 'daylight_linking' FAIL critical. (4) controls.lamp_efficacy_lm_per_w=15 ≥ 95 → 15 < 95 FAIL critical. Three of four sub-checks FAIL → Rule 6 FAIL with non_compliance_flags entries per validator.md instruction.

decisions.label: Efficacy 15 lm/W ≪ 95 lm/W (INV-6 sub-check 4 FAIL)
decisions.summary: 50W GU10 halogen produces 750 initial lm = 15 lm/W. Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) §6 Table 6.2 sets the office luminaire efficacy target at 95 lm/W (general office display lighting). Remediation: replace each lamp with GU10 LED retrofit at 8W / 800 lm = 100 lm/W (PASS), keeping the existing fitting body + transformer + cabling. Connected lighting load drops 2500 W → 400 W (84 % saving) with negligible disruption.
decisions.rule: Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) §6 Table 6.2 lamp efficacy ≥ 95 lm/W
decisions.code_clause: Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) §6 Table 6.2 + BS EN 15193-1:2017 § 6

decisions.label: No daylight-linking despite W glazing (INV-6 sub-check 3 FAIL)
decisions.summary: Column 0 (L01, L11, L21, L31, L41 at x=300) sits within 1500 mm of the glazed W wall. Part L 2021 §6 requires daylight-linked dimming on those fittings. Remediation: split column 0 into perimeter zone Z1 on 0-10V dimming with photocell feedback at the W window head.

decisions.label: No occupancy detection (INV-6 sub-check 2 FAIL)
decisions.summary: Private office requires automatic presence-with-absence-detection per Part L 2021 §6. Remediation: add PIR sensor wired to the C-L02..C-L05 interior circuits with manual override via SW01.

sections.title: S/H ratio check (PASS — halogen wash density)
sections.summary: 50 luminaires in 5×10 grid in 8000×6000 room, 300 mm edge clearance. S_x=(8000−600)/9=822 mm. S_y=(6000−600)/4=1350 mm. Hm=1950 mm. SHR_max=1.4 (LED_DOWNLIGHT narrow-beam ontology default; halogen GU10 ≤ 1.4 also). Limit=1.4×1950=2730 mm. S_x=822 ≤ 2730 PASS (30 % of limit); S_y=1350 ≤ 2730 PASS (49 % of limit). INV-2 PASS comfortably — the halogen wash density is far denser than SHR would allow at minimum.

sections.title: Zone assignment (INV-7 PASS — but the perimeter gap is a Part L issue)
sections.summary: All 50 luminaires in single Z2 interior zone with control='manual'. inputs.glazed_walls=['west'] non-empty. zones[] contains NO perimeter zone — INV-7 Rule 3 contrapositive ('if glazed=[], no perimeter zone') trivially PASSes because there IS a glazed wall but the structural test only forbids the reverse case. INV-7 PASS structurally. However the missing perimeter zone IS a Part L violation, surfaced by INV-6 sub-check 3 (daylight_linking absent). Two INVs cooperate: INV-7 confirms no broken zone definitions; INV-6 catches the missing zone.

sections.title: Switch + circuit topology (INV-3 + INV-4 + INV-5 PASS)
sections.summary: Single S-wall entrance + controls_protocol=switched → 1 switch required, 1 emitted (SW01 1-gang at S latch side). INV-3 PASS. 5 row circuits C-L01..C-L05, each carrying 10 fittings × 50 W = 500 W on 6A Type B MCB (500 ≤ 1104 W = 0.8 × 6 × 230) — INV-5 PASS at 45 % of headroom (limiting factor is lamp load not OCPD). Each circuit's luminaires share single row_index → INV-4 PASS (no Z-pattern). Homeruns all on W wall.

sections.title: Composite INV-10 FAIL
sections.summary: Schema validation PASSED. non_compliance_flags[] populated with 3 severity=critical entries (efficacy + daylight + occupancy — all three reference Part L). Rule 10 sub-check 3 satisfied: controls.part_l_compliant=false AND ≥1 non_compliance_flags entry with severity=critical referencing Part L. But the layout is non-compliant overall (compliant=false) so INV-10 composite FAIL HIGH reflects the failed state honestly. Downstream consumers reading intent-out.json see controls_summary.part_l_compliant=false and skip Part L sign-off gating.

sections.title: Remediation summary (engineer notes)
sections.summary: Three changes needed for Part L sign-off: (1) GU10 LED retrofit lamps (8W/800lm/100 lm/W) cutting connected load 84 % — cheapest fix, retains fitting bodies. (2) Split column 0 into perimeter zone Z1 with 0-10V dimming + photocell at W window head. (3) Add PIR sensor wired to interior circuits with manual override via SW01. Total cost estimate: ~£3-5k for 50-fitting office (lamp swap + photocell + PIR + new dimming-capable driver on Z1). Payback period <2 years from energy savings alone (2100 W reduction × 2500 hours/year × £0.30/kWh = £1575/year).

sections.title: v1.7 retrofit
sections.summary: D5 retrofit (2026-06-04): zone Z2 receives purpose='task' (ZP-01 default — the only valid mapping for a private_office room with a 300 lx Em target) and em_target_lux=300 (mirrors existing calculation_summary.target_illuminance_lux). All 50 GU10 halogen downlights receive mount_type='recessed' (MT-01 default — recessed downlight by physical definition). per_zone_achieved[] populated with one entry for Z2: target 300 lx, achieved 343 lx, ratio_compliance='pass'. Critically, the Part-L FAIL state is PRESERVED VERBATIM: controls.part_l_compliant=false, 3 critical non_compliance_flags, INV-6 FAIL, INV-10 FAIL — D5 additions (zone purpose + mount_type + per-zone achievement) are orthogonal to Part L 2021 §6 efficacy/controls compliance. This example becomes the canonical demonstration that photometric INV-19 PASS coexists with regulatory INV-6 FAIL.

decisions.label: Default purpose='task' for Z2 (ZP-01)
decisions.summary: v1.6.0 example pre-dates the §4.2.2 area split. Backwards-compat default ZP-01 maps the single 'Interior' zone to purpose='task' — the only valid interpretation for a private_office room with a 300 lx Em target. em_target_lux=300 mirrors existing calculation_summary.target_illuminance_lux unchanged.

decisions.label: Default mount_type='recessed' (MT-01)
decisions.summary: Existing example specifies 50W GU10 halogen recessed downlights — recessed by physical definition. MT-01 default chosen; z_mm + suspension_length_mm omitted per recessed convention (inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700).

decisions.label: Part-L FAIL state preserved (D5 orthogonal to INV-6)
decisions.summary: INV-6 sub-checks evaluate lamp_efficacy_lm_per_w, controls.occupancy_sensing, controls.daylight_linking, controls.part_l_assessed — none of these intersect with the new D5 fields (zones[].purpose, zones[].em_target_lux, luminaires[].mount_type, per_zone_achieved[]). The 3 critical non_compliance_flags and INV-6/INV-10 FAIL verdicts remain UNCHANGED. Photometric INV-19 PASS (343 ≥ 300) is the design's structural soundness; the regulatory Part-L FAIL is the design's compliance gap.
decisions.code_clause: Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) § 6 + BS EN 12464-1:2021 § 4.2.2

invariants.evidence: Lumen method: N=50 halogen downlights × 750 lm × UF=0.55 × MF=0.80 / 48 m² = achieved_illuminance_lux=343.75 lux ≥ target 300 lux (Rule 1 PASS). 14.6 % headroom. PASS achieved by brute-force fitting count (50 fittings, 1 per m²); a principled LED-based design would reach 300 lux with ~5 fittings at 1000 lm + 100 lm/W. Per [spacing-rules#lumen-method-formula] + ontology LED_DOWNLIGHT UF table at RI=1.5 (proxy for halogen GU10 similar geometry).

S/H ratio: Hm=1950 mm, SHR_max=1.4 (LED_DOWNLIGHT narrow-beam ontology default), limit=2730 mm. 10 cols × 5 rows grid: S_x=(8000−600)/9=822 mm ≤ 2730 PASS (30 % of limit); S_y=(6000−600)/4=1350 mm ≤ 2730 PASS (49 % of limit). Per [spacing-rules#shr-max-default] + CIBSE LG7 §6.2. PASS comfortably — halogen wash density is far denser than SHR minimum.

Switch coverage: 1 entrance declared (S wall, offset 3500, width 900, door_swing=inward_latch_right) + controls_protocol=switched → INV-3 Rule 1 sub-check requires switches.length ≥ entrances.length: 1 ≥ 1 PASS. SW01 1-gang at (3500, 200) sits 200 mm to the latch side at door's left frame, controls_circuit='C-L01,C-L02,C-L03,C-L04,C-L05' (all five rows from one switch). height_aff_mm=1200 within [1150, 1250] band per [switching-rules#height] (BS 7671 §553.1.1 + IET OSG App E §E1.4). switch_side='latch'. Rule 3 PASS.

Circuit topology: 5 row circuits C-L01..C-L05 each feed exactly one row of 10 luminaires sharing single row_index (0, 1, 2, 3, 4 respectively). max(row_indices) − min(row_indices) = 0 per circuit ≤ 1 → no Z-pattern, no diagonal jumps. homerun_endpoints all on W wall (x_mm=0) at row y_mm ∈ {300, 1650, 3000, 4350, 5700} — all four required keys (x_mm, y_mm, wall) present, wall ∈ {N,S,E,W}. Rule 4a + 4b PASS.

Circuit load: each of C-L01..C-L05 has total_load_w=500 W = 10 × 50 W ≤ 0.8 × 6 A × 230 V = 1104 W (6A MCB limit). All five circuits PASS the 80% continuous-load cap per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §433.1.1 + IET OSG App A. 500 W is 45 % of headroom each — limiting factor is the inherited halogen lamp wattage rather than the OCPD.

Part L compliance: inputs.new_build=true (major refurbishment triggers Part L 2021 §6) AND jurisdiction=england_wales triggers enforcement. (1) controls.part_l_assessed=true PASS. (2) controls.required=[] does NOT include 'occupancy' → sub-check 2 FAIL. (3) glazed_walls=['west'] non-empty AND controls.required does NOT include 'daylight_linking' → sub-check 3 FAIL. (4) controls.lamp_efficacy_lm_per_w=15 < 95 → sub-check 4 FAIL critical (efficacy target per Approved Doc L (Part L 2021) §6 Table 6.2). Three of four sub-checks FAIL → Rule 6 FAIL. Per validator.md: emit non_compliance_flags entries with severity=critical AND set INV to FAIL — three entries emitted. controls.part_l_compliant=false. Per Approved Doc L 2021 §6 + BS EN 15193-1:2017 §6.

Zone assignment: all 50 luminaires reference zone_id=Z2 (interior); 1:1 referential integrity. zones[]=[Z2] only — no perimeter zone declared. inputs.glazed_walls=['west'] non-empty, but INV-7 Rule 3 only forbids the case where zone_type='perimeter' exists without matching glazed wall, OR perimeter exists when glazed_walls=[]. The structural check trivially PASSes here. The missing perimeter zone IS a Part L violation, surfaced by INV-6 sub-check 3 — INV-6 + INV-7 cooperate to catch both the structural (INV-7) and the regulatory (INV-6) layer.

selection_source.photometric_source='ontology_default'; citation references 'CIBSE LG7 §6.2 (recessed downlight typical values; narrow beam) + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4' — matches ontology LED_DOWNLIGHT.photometric._citation as the photometric geometry proxy. Lumen + efficacy fields override ontology defaults with the actual halogen specification. The override is honest (engineer notes the substitution in selection_source.citation). Rule 8 PASS.

drafting_furniture present with all 4 required blocks (title_block + scale_bar + dimensions[2] + luminaire_schedule). All annotations declare font_family='Arial' + explicit font_size_pt ∈ {8, 10}. No {{placeholder}} substrings in any text field. dimensions.length=2 ≥ 2 minimum. Rule 9 PASS.

Schema validation PASSED (precondition: golden CI gate). All top-level required fields populated. non_compliance_flags[] populated with 3 entries, all correct object shape {message, reference, severity=critical}, all three reference Approved Doc L 2021 §6. Rule 10 sub-check 3 cross-check: controls.part_l_compliant=false AND ≥1 non_compliance_flags entry with severity=critical referencing Part L — PASS (three such entries). But the layout is non-compliant overall (calculation_summary.compliant=false, with INV-6 FAIL) so INV-10 composite FAIL HIGH per the populated non_compliance_flags + compliant=false signalling pair.

Photometric cascade resolved: consumed_intents.photometric_grid sourced from cascade-uk-part-l-fail-incandescent/intent-out.json. payload.task_area_compliant=true; payload.achieved_avg_illuminance_lux=564 vs target=500 (sub-check 3 PASS — engineer over-provisioned 50 halogen fittings to compensate for 15 lm/W lamp efficacy); 0 non_compliance_flags cascaded to lighting-layout calculation_summary (sub-check 4 PASS, vacuously). Engineering nuance: photometric INV-11 PASSes (brute-force fitting count delivers lux on the working plane) — the Part-L INV-6 FAIL remains structurally INDEPENDENT (Part L 2021 §6 efficacy target is a regulation, not a photometric grid measurement). This is the canonical 'photometrically compliant but regulatorily non-compliant' refurb. Cascade per skill.manifest.json consumes_intents trigger 'mode == full_drawing'.

Zone purpose required + valid. Zones inspected: 1. Per-zone purpose: Z2: purpose=task, em_target_lux=300. Orphan-surrounding check: pass (no surrounding zones present, so check vacuous). All zones declare purpose ∈ {task, surrounding, background, circulation}. INV-13 PASS. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.1 (task area) + area-definitions.json §4.2.2.1.

Surrounding ratio compliance. Surrounding zones inspected: 0. No zones declare purpose='surrounding' — INV-14 vacuous PASS per validator §INV-14 vacuous condition. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.2 + Table 6 (area-definitions.json table_6_ratio_rules.task_to_surrounding).

Background floor. Background zones inspected: 0. No zones declare purpose='background' — INV-15 vacuous PASS per validator §INV-15 vacuous condition. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.3 + Table 6 (area-definitions.json table_6_ratio_rules.task_to_background).

mount_type ↔ z_mm/suspension consistency. Luminaires inspected: 50. Pendant geometry checks: 0 pendant luminaires. Suspended geometry checks: 0 suspended luminaires. Recessed/surface/track: 50 luminaires (all 50 GU10 halogen downlights are mount_type='recessed'), z_mm omitted per MT-01 convention (recessed inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700). INV-16 vacuous PASS — no pendant/suspended geometry to verify. Citation: BS EN 60598-2-2 (recessed) + mount-type-rules.yaml MT-01.

Ceiling clearance + working-plane floor. Working plane reference: 750 mm AFF. Per-luminaire floor check: all 50 luminaires are recessed; z_mm not declared and inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700 per MT-01 convention. Inherited z=2700 > working_plane=750 (clearance=1950 mm). Per-luminaire ceiling clearance: recessed luminaires are physically inset into ceiling, suspension_length_mm=0 implied; sum=2700 ≤ ceiling=2700 PASS. INV-17 PASS — no luminaire below task plane, no luminaire exceeds ceiling. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 + BS EN 60598-2 + MT-04.

hm_mm derivation consistency. Room hm_mm recorded: 1950 mm. Lowest pendant/suspended z_mm: N/A (no pendant/suspended — all 50 luminaires recessed). Recessed branch: expected hm_mm = ceiling_height_mm − working_plane_mm = 2700 − 750 = 1950 mm. Drift: |1950 − 1950| = 0 mm, tolerance ±50 mm. INV-18 PASS. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 + repo derivation convention.

Per-zone achievement. Zones inspected: 1. Per-zone achievement: Z2 (task): target=300, achieved=343, gap=-43, gap_pct=-14.3%, ratio_compliance=pass, severity=none (achieved ≥ target). Aggregate: 1 PASS, 0 marginal, 0 MEDIUM, 0 HIGH. INV-19 PASS. Engineering nuance: photometric/INV-19 PASS coexists with regulatory/INV-6 FAIL — the Part L 2021 §6 efficacy + controls regime is INDEPENDENT of the BS EN 12464-1 Em target compliance check. The layout meets 300 lx by brute-force fitting count (50 halogen fittings, 1/m²) but ships 2500 W of connected lighting load against a Part L 95 lm/W efficacy target — this orthogonality is what INV-6 catches and INV-19 does not. Citation: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.1 + Table 5 (lux-levels.json private_office entry, Em=500 lx; example uses 300 lx per the v1.6.0 brief which retains the inherited 'general office lighting' Table 5.3 lower-bound for refurb scope).

title_block.project_name: UK private office — D3.C.2 Part L halogen-refurb FAIL demo
